User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account within the system, used for authentication, authorization, and tracking actions. Assumes an external authentication system manages sensitive credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for login and notifications.

role.description: Assigned role determining the user's permissions and access level within the application (e.g., 'admin', 'operator').

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Manages detailed information for each customer, including contact details, pool/equipment specifications, and financial status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity. Can be automatically generated or a custom ID like CUIT/DNI.

telefono.description: Primary contact phone number for the client.

direccion.description: Street address of the client or service location.

localidad.description: City or locality of the client.

provincia.description: Province or state of the client.

mail.description: Email address of the client for communication and notifications.

cuitDni.description: Client's national identification number (CUIT or DNI) for legal or fiscal purposes.

observaciones.description: Long-form text field for any additional notes or observations about the client.

medidasPileta.description: Description of the pool's dimensions or measurements (e.g., '8x4x1.5m').

volumen.description: Estimated or calculated volume of the pool in cubic meters or liters.

dosisCloro.description: Recommended or typical chlorine dose for the client's pool.

cantidadBidones.description: Number of chlorine drums/containers typically supplied or needed for the client's pool.

modeloEquipo.description: Model of the main pool equipment installed at the client's location (e.g., pump, filter).

enComodato.description: Indicates if the main equipment is provided to the client on a consignment or loan basis.

esClienteReposicion.description: Flag indicating if the client is part of the regular chlorine replenishment service.

saldoActual.description: The client's current outstanding balance or credit amount. This field is typically calculated but stored for quick access and real-time monitoring.

fechaUltimaReposicion.description: Date and time of the client's last chlorine replenishment, used for scheduling and reminders.

creadoPorId.description: Reference to the User who initially created this client record. (Relationship: User 1:N Client)

fechaCreacion.description: Date and time when the client record was created.

ultimaModificacionPorId.description: Reference to the User who last modified this client record. (Relationship: User 1:N Client)

fechaUltimaModificacion.description: Date and time when the client record was last modified.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible by the user themselves and administrators. This path also implicitly serves as a base for authorization by 'request.auth.uid'.
Marker collection to identify users with 'admin' privileges. The existence of a document for a specific userId grants admin access. This follows the DBAC 'Existence over Content' principle.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has the 'admin' role.

definition.description: Marker collection to identify users with 'operator' privileges. The existence of a document for a specific userId grants operator access. This follows the DBAC 'Existence over Content' principle.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who has the 'operator' role.

definition.description: Stores detailed information for each customer. Access is controlled by 'admin' or 'operator' roles. Includes denormalized 'saldoActual' and 'fechaUltimaReposicion' for quick lookup and efficient security rules for notifications/reports. 'creadoPorId' and 'ultimaModificacionPorId' are for auditability.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client, which can be custom (e.g., CUIT/DNI) or auto-generated.

definition.description: Manages details of products offered. Admins can create/update/delete products, while operators and admins can read them. This collection adheres to homogeneous security posture.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a product.

definition.description: Manages details of services offered. Admins can create/update/delete services, while operators and admins can read them. This collection adheres to homogeneous security posture.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a service.

definition.description: Manages various financial accounts (e.g., bank, cash). Admins can create/update/delete, operators and admins can read/update balances. Homogeneous security posture.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for a financial account.

definition.description: Records all business transactions (sales, payments, services, etc.). Operators and Admins can create and read transactions. Includes references to 'clientId', 'productId/serviceId', 'financialAccountId', and 'creadoPorId' for linking and auditability. Homogeneous security posture.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a transaction record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for ChloriTrack Pro is designed to prioritize authorization independence, clear access modeling, and efficient query capabilities, adhering strictly to the provided core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design achieves authorization independence by avoiding any hierarchical authorization dependencies that would require `get()` calls in security rules. Instead, authorization is primarily based on two mechanisms:
1.  **Path-Based Ownership for Users:** The `/users/{userId}` path ensures that a user's own profile data is directly accessible via their `request.auth.uid`, making authorization trivial (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
2.  **Role-Based Access Control (DBAC) via Existence Checks:** For application-wide data (clients, products, financial accounts, transactions), access is granted based on the existence of a marker document in dedicated role collections, specifically `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_operator/{userId}`. For example, to allow an operator to read clients, the rule would be `allow read: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_operator/$(request.auth.uid));`. This completely eliminates the need to traverse the database hierarchy or fetch parent documents to determine access rights, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

Fields like `creadoPorId` and `ultimaModificacionPorId` in documents like `Client` are included for auditability and tracking, not as primary authorization mechanisms. The `Client` entity also includes denormalized `saldoActual` and `fechaUltimaReposicion` to facilitate quick lookups for financial monitoring and notification features without requiring complex aggregations or `get()` calls, adhering to the Invariants principle.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure enables secure `list` operations by implementing **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture)**. All documents within a given collection (e.g., `/clients`, `/products`, `/financial_accounts`, `/transactions`) share the same security requirements. There are no mixed-security-posture documents within a single collection (e.g., public and private clients mixed). This allows Firestore security rules to grant `list` access based on simple, efficient conditions (like checking the user's role via an `exists()` function), guaranteeing that only authorized documents are returned without requiring Firestore to filter results after retrieving them. For example, an operator can `list` all clients because all clients in the `/clients` collection are intended to be visible to operators.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** User profiles are private to the user at `/users/{userId}`.
*   **Global Roles:** Implemented using marker documents at `/roles_admin/{userId}` and `/roles_operator/{userId}` for efficient DBAC.
*   **Company-Wide Data (Collaborative/Shared by Roles):** Clients, products, services, financial accounts, and transactions are stored in top-level collections (`/clients`, `/products`, etc.) and are accessible based on the authenticated user's role (admin or operator). No `members` map is needed within these documents because access is not per-document-collaboration but rather a role-based capability to access the entire collection.
